notes.text: Use the three-condition date overlap for concurrent claims
Include Medicare FFS concurrent claims — not just APCD
Professional-only events still get a facility assignment attempt via NPI matching
In-state classification is based on successful assignment from the known facility list — not the state field. State field can be null, typoed, or otherwise wrong for valid AR events.
Flow counts must track: total events (including professional-only) → assigned vs. unassigned → of unassigned: professional-only / out-of-state / no match found
Step 4: verify no event has been assigned to more than one hospital before proceeding
Carry forward available variables in earlier steps to avoid unnecessary joins later
Run each section independently — do not run end-to-end first
Check commented-out row counts; note any discrepancies (may just be a stale re-run count)
Pharmacy deduplication in Step 1 is intentionally complex — review carefully
Mom/child linkage: child needs an AR birth record; mom needs MPID
Foster care exclusion applies across the full time period of interest — not just at enrollment
One mom can only appear once: first HS child is the child of interest, or first non-HS child if no HS sibling
Mom coverage requirement: 3+ months medical AND pharmacy in the year prior to child's first wellness visit, AND 3+ months of each remaining in the study period after that visit
Codes of interest: F630* and Z726*
2024: commercial/MCD only — this is the Medicare FFS offset year, no FFS data for 2024
2021–2023: commercial/MCD + Medicare FFS + combined totals for all three years
Counts are member-level (newid), not person-level — note this explicitly in all output. The same person may appear more than once across member IDs.
Macro-ize the year variable — all years should run through identical logic
Provider analysis: assess whether claims concentrate with a small set of providers or distribute broadly across many
SSDrive must point to a dedicated project folder — not SASWORK. These must be on separate drives.
Do not use NODUPKEY to select highest income tier — use FIRST. logic after a proper sort by studyid and descending income_rank
Pull medical records first per year, then dental member records into a separate dental plans table
Apply ranking so each standalone dental plan maps to exactly one medical plan type
Adult/child assignment: as of Jan 1 of the year OR enrollment start date, whichever is later
Run analysis on the regular member table — not the enrollment segment table
